tweet_text: day 4. 6 revenue streams live. content farm posted 312 pieces across 3 niches while i was asleep. woke up to 47 new followers and 2 affiliate clicks. the machine doesn't sleep
found a tool that scrapes every new product hunt launch and emails me the founder's contact info. been cold emailing them offering growth services. 14% reply rate. $0 tool cost
cold emailed 200 dentists with a mockup of a website i already built for them. personalized screenshot in every email. 27% open rate. 4 calls booked. closing at $1,500 each
week 2 numbers: 8 platforms posting automatically. 1,247 total posts. 3 affiliate sales ($127). 1 cold email client ($500). not rich yet but the infrastructure is built and compounding
everyone wants to find the perfect method. there is no perfect method. there's just executing 10 methods at once and seeing which 2 actually work. then you double down on those 2
how i set up automated content for 3 niches in one afternoon: 1. wrote 50 posts per niche (3 hours) 2. loaded into buffer (20 min) 3. scheduled 30 days out (10 min) 4. moved on to the next thing
everyone's chasing the next AI wrapper. i'm building boring content farms that post 100x/day across faith fitness and tech niches. wrappers die. distribution compounds forever
automated my competitor monitoring. visualping.io watches 200+ pages. they change pricing i know in 30 seconds. they launch a feature i reverse engineer it by lunch. $0/mo free tier
day 7. first paying customer from cold email. $500 for a site i templated in 45 minutes. margins are insane when you pre-build the product before the outreach
the gap between $0/mo and $3k/mo is about 90 days of unhinged execution. most people spend those 90 days planning. then they plan for another 90 days. then they quit
how i find what to build: 1. go to product hunt 2. find apps making $10k+/mo 3. build a niche version (faith, fitness, students) 4. use MIT repos as starter code 5. ship in a weekend
cold dm results this week: sent 340. got 89 replies. booked 12 calls. closed 3 at $500 each. $1,500 from mass dms. not elegant but it prints
month 1 dashboard: 20 revenue streams launched. 4 generating revenue. $3,241 total. content farm: $847. cold email: $1,500. affiliate: $412. digital products: $482. scaling the top 4 now
built 3 newsletter landing pages yesterday. each one targets a different niche. beehiiv free tier. total cost: $0. total time: 4 hours. first subscribers already coming in from reddit posts
buffer + canva + chatgpt + 2 hours = 30 days of scheduled content across 4 platforms. people overthink this. batch it once. forget it. move on to revenue
unpopular take: you don't need a unique idea. you need a unique distribution strategy. 10 people can sell the same notion template. the one who cold dms 500 creators wins
the cold email stack that actually works: instantly.ai for warmup ($30/mo) + apollo for leads ($0 free tier) + my template that books calls. total: $30/mo. revenue last month: $2,000
shipped my first gumroad product at 11pm. a $19 notion template. woke up to 3 sales. $57 while sleeping. not life changing but proof the system works. now i make 20 more
stop building features. start building distribution. my worst app with great distribution makes more than my best app with zero distribution. every single time
how i reverse engineer any successful creator's funnel: 1. sign up for their email list 2. screenshot every email for 14 days 3. map the sequence 4. copy the structure 5. adapt for my niche. free MBA
ran a test. same exact product. one listed on gumroad with 0 marketing. one posted to 5 subreddits with value posts. gumroad alone: 0 sales. reddit + gumroad: 11 sales in 3 days
my content farm posted 47 times yesterday across 3 accounts. i didn't touch any of them. one account gained 230 followers. the other two flatlined. killing those two. tripling down on the winner
the meta right now: ai-generated short form clips posted to tiktok, youtube shorts, and instagram reels simultaneously. one piece of content, 3 platforms, automated. 100 clips/day if you want
every piece of research i do becomes: 1 twitter thread, 3 standalone tweets, 1 newsletter issue, 1 gumroad product outline, and 5 reddit posts. one input, 11 outputs. zero waste
day 14 update. 3 cold email campaigns running. 1 content farm profitable. 2 gumroad products listed. 1 newsletter at 340 subscribers. total revenue: $4,100. total spend: $67. the math is stupid
emailbison.com warms up inboxes automatically. went from 12% to 43% open rates in 2 weeks. if your cold emails aren't landing it's probably deliverability not your copy
people spend 6 months picking a niche. i launched in 3 niches simultaneously in one weekend. 2 failed. 1 is making money. that took 48 hours vs 6 months of analysis paralysis
the notion template that sells itself: 1. find a subreddit with 500k+ members 2. give away 80% of the template as a free post 3. link the full version on gumroad for $19 4. repeat in 10 subreddits

week 3 content farm stats: account 1 (faith niche): 2,340 followers, $0 revenue yet. account 2 (fitness): 890 followers, 3 affiliate sales ($67). account 3 (tech): 4,100 followers, 1 sponsorship inquiry
built a python script that generates 50 social posts from one blog article. different hooks, different formats, different lengths. one afternoon of writing = 6 months of content
i don't understand why people build products before they have distribution. build the audience first. then ask them what to buy. then build that. the order matters

how i got my first 1,000 newsletter subscribers: 1. wrote 5 high-value reddit posts in target subreddits 2. included lead magnet link 3. beehiiv captured emails 4. automated welcome sequence. total cost: $0
cold email is just paid ads with 0 ad spend. you're still putting a message in front of strangers. but instead of paying meta $3/click you're paying $0. i will never understand why people skip it
the flywheel: content builds audience. audience validates products. products fund ads. ads build more audience. i'm at step 2. most people are stuck at step 0 (planning step 1)
apollo.io free tier gives you 10,000 leads per month. that's 10,000 people you can email about your product for $0. if you're not using this you're leaving money everywhere
sent 500 cold emails last week. got 43 replies. 11 positive. 7 calls booked. 2 closed at $750 each. $1,500 from 500 emails. cost per acquisition: $0 and some time

30 day check: total revenue $6,847 across 5 methods. best performer: cold email services ($3,000). worst: ai music ($0). killing ai music. doubling cold email capacity. this is how you find product market fit

most people treat cold outreach like a lottery ticket. i treat it like a math problem. send 100 emails. get 8% reply. close 20% of replies. average deal $500. that's $800 per 100 emails. now send 1,000

how i pre-build websites for cold email prospects: 1. pick a niche (dentists, plumbers, etc) 2. build one template site 3. swap logo/name per prospect in 2 min 4. screenshot it 5. include screenshot in cold email. 31% open rate
controversial: info products are the highest margin business that exists. $0 COGS. $0 inventory. $0 shipping. write once sell forever. a $29 pdf has better margins than most VC-funded startups
just discovered clay.com. it enriches any lead list with 50+ data points automatically. feeding this into my cold email personalization. open rates about to jump from 27% to 40%+
the content farm hit a milestone. 10,000 total posts across all accounts. average engagement rate: 2.3%. top performing account: 4.7%. that top account is now getting brand deal inquiries
your first $1,000 online is the hardest. it took me 34 days. your first $5,000 is the second hardest. it took me 23 more days. after that the system compounds and the math just works

how i a/b test everything: 1. write 3 versions of every cold email 2. send 50 of each 3. measure open + reply rates after 48 hours 4. kill bottom 2 5. iterate on winner 6. repeat. my templates get better every week

found 47 notion template creators on twitter making $2k+/mo. all of them have the same funnel: free template on reddit > email list > paid template on gumroad > upsell to course. now i'm building this exact funnel x3
daily dashboard: cold emails sent: 200. replies: 17. calls booked: 4. content pieces posted: 47. new followers: 312. affiliate clicks: 23. revenue today: $750. the dashboard doesn't lie
everyone's trying to go viral. i'm trying to go consistent. 50 posts/day across 5 accounts for 90 days = 13,500 posts. some of them will hit. it's math not luck
tool stack that runs my entire operation: buffer ($15/mo) + beehiiv ($0) + gumroad ($0) + apollo ($0) + instantly ($30/mo) + canva ($0 free tier). total monthly: $45. revenue: 150x that

how i find clients who will pay $1,500 for a basic website: 1. google [city] [business type]" 2. find businesses with terrible or no websites 3. build them one on a template in 20 min 4. cold email with a screenshot of their new site. they call you"

the overnight build session shipped: 3 gumroad products. 200 scheduled tweets. 5 reddit posts. 2 cold email campaigns. 1 newsletter issue. if you're sleeping 8 hours a night you're leaving money on the table (but also sleep is important don't be dumb)
